content: Remote OK $100k starting bonus with up $56 WRVU ED Overnight Teleradiology 10 and 8 hour options American Society of Neuroradiology <strong>Expand Show Other Jobs<br><br></strong>Job Saved Save Job<br><br>$100k starting bonus with up to $56 WRVU ED Overnight Teleradiology Remote, 10- and 8-hour options<br><br>Orlando Health Medical Group<br><br>Facebook X LinkedIn Share<br><br>Application<br><br>First Name<br><br>Last Name<br><br>Email address<br><br>Message To Employer (required)<br><br>Please attach your resume and up to two additional documents.<br><br>Files must be 2MB or less and acceptable file types.<br><br>Add File<br><br><strong>Captcha / Bot Check<br><br></strong>Copy from here<br><br><ul><li>to here<br><br></li></ul>Click to hear these letters.<br><br>What is this?<br><br>The application opened in a new tab.<br><br>You may close this popup.<br><br>By using this feature you agree to our Terms and Conditions and Privacy Policy.<br><br>Details<br><br><strong>Posted:</strong> 08-Jun-26<br><br><strong>Location:</strong> Orlando, Florida<br><br><strong>Type:</strong> Full Time<br><br><strong>Subspecialties<br><br></strong>Diagnostic Radiology<br><br>Emergency Radiology<br><br>Interventional Radiology<br><br>Musculoskeletal Radiology<br><br>Neuroradiology<br><br>Non-Specialty Specific<br><br>Nuclear Medicine<br><br><strong>Additional Information<br><br></strong>Hybrid/Remote is allowed.<br><br><strong>7 on/14 off Remote Overnight Emergency Imaging Radiologist with Orlando Health, Nationally Ranked Hospital<br><br></strong>Orlando Health Medical Group is seeking well-trained and motivated radiologists to provide <strong>overnight Emergency imaging coverage</strong>. The ideal candidate is comfortable with all modalities of general body imaging and stroke imaging including CTA and CTP. Our Emergency Radiologists oversee all aspects of diagnostic imaging for acutely ill and injured patients. Our imaging spans all organ systems and modalities including X-ray, CT, and MRI. Candidates must have a demonstrated career interest in Emergency Radiology with excellent clinical skills in all modalities and organ systems. Neuroradiology expertise is highly regarded but not required. This position works closely with a team of experienced radiologists equally sharing the work covering STAT exams and non-subspecialty routine studies with opportunity to participate in clinical research and resident education. Prior experience in Emergency Radiology is preferred. Candidate must be able to thrive in 7 on, and 14 off overnight schedule.<br><br><strong>Candidate Should Expect<br><br></strong><ul><li>Candidates can choose between doing 10- or 8-hour shifts</li><li>Extremely competitive base compensation with a $56 WRVU</li><li>$100k starting bonus</li><li>Working within an all-inclusive, high-volume group</li><li>Utilizing the EpicÂ© comprehensive health record keeping system</li><li>Phillips PACS system</li><li>CT, MRI, Body Imaging, Emergency Radiology, Neuroradiology, General Radiology</li><li>Not a J-1 visa sponsored position.<br><br></li></ul>Orlando Health Medical Group performs over 3 million encounters annually across more than 200 ambulatory locations. The facilities our Medical Group serves includes including Orlando Regional Medical Center, a Level I trauma center, Arnold Palmer Hospital for Children, Winnie Palmer Hospital for Women &amp; Babies, Orlando Health Cancer Institute, Dr. P. Phillips Hospital, South Seminole Hospital, South Lake Hospital, St. Cloud Regional Medical Center, Orlando Healthâs Health Central Hospital and Bayfront Health Hospital. Medical Group Radiology<br><br></strong>With the 2022 acquisition of Medical Center Radiology Group (MCRG), whose board-certified radiologists have interpreted medical imaging for the healthcare Orlando Health Medical Group Radiology is the diagnostic radiology, interventional radiology and nuclear medicine group for all Orlando Health hospitals as well as the systemâs numerous freestanding ERs and Orlando Health Imaging Centers outpatient locations throughout Central Florida, with many modalities accredited by the American College of Radiology.
